steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content: The three interior angles of any triangle sum to $180°$. We have $\angle X = 23°$ and $\angle Y = 66°$, so we need to find $\angle Z$.

steps.title: Solve for angle Z
steps.content:
Set up the equation: $$23° + 66° + \angle Z = 180°$$
Combine the known angles: $$89° + \angle Z = 180°$$
Subtract $89°$ from both sides: $$\angle Z = 91°$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
